paper_title: Cosmogenic Nuclide Dating and Ebro Terrace Succession

methods.description: This method involves using cosmogenic nuclides to date geological surfaces by analyzing their concentration profiles. It includes exposure depth profiles, simple burial, and P-PINI techniques to provide chronological constraints.

steps.description: Collect samples from geological surfaces for cosmogenic nuclide analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect geological samples for cosmogenic nuclide analysis

steps.description: Analyze the concentration of in situ-produced cosmogenic nuclides in the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze cosmogenic nuclide concentration in samples

steps.description: Apply exposure depth profiles, simple burial, and P-PINI techniques to interpret the nuclide concentration data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply dating techniques to interpret nuclide data

methods.description: This method involves mapping and correlating terrace sequences across the Ebro drainage basin using geomorphological and geological maps, as well as published works.

steps.description: Obtain georeferenced geomorphological maps and digital geological maps for the Ebro basin.
steps.searchable_summary: download geomorphological and geological maps for Ebro basin

steps.description: Map terraces using the obtained maps and manually retrace contours where necessary.
steps.searchable_summary: map terraces and retrace contours from maps

steps.description: Correlate terrace sequences across different valleys using published works and map data.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate terrace sequences using maps and literature

steps.description: Validate terrace correlations using ages and magnetic polarities from literature.
steps.searchable_summary: validate terrace correlations with age and polarity data
